created.description: Timestamp when the schema was created

base.description:
All Okta-defined profile properties are defined in a profile subschema with the resolution scope `#base`. You can't modify these properties, except to update permissions, to change the nullability of `firstName` and `lastName`, or to specify a pattern for `login`. They can't be removed.

The base user profile is based on the [System for Cross-domain Identity Management: Core Schema](https://tools.

enum.description:
Enumerated value of the property.

The value of the property is limited to one of the values specified in the enum definition. The list of values for the enum must consist of unique elements.

externalName.description:
Name of the property as it exists in an external application

**NOTE**: When you add a custom property, only Identity Provider app user schemas require `externalName` to be
included in the request body. If an existing custom Identity Provider app user schema property has an empty
`externalName`, requests aren't allowed to update other properties until the `externalName` is defined.

maxLength.description: Maximum character length of a string property

minLength.description: Minimum character length of a string property

oneOf.description:
Non-empty array of valid JSON schemas. The `oneOf` key is only supported in conjunction with `enum` and provides a mechanism to return a display name for the `enum` value.
Each schema has the following format: ``` { "const": "enumValue", "title": "display name" } ``` When `enum` is used in conjunction with `oneOf`, you must keep the set of enumerated values and their order.
For exam

pattern.description: For `string` property types, specifies the regular expression used to validate the property

permissions.description: Access control permissions for the property

required.description: Determines whether the property is required

title.description: User-defined display name for the property

unique.description: Determines whether property values must be unique

enum.description:
Enumerated value of the property.

The value of the property is limited to one of the values specified in the enum definition. The list of values for the enum must consist of unique elements.

included in the request body. If an existing custom Identity Provider app user schema property has an empty
`externalName`, requests aren't allowed to update other properties until the `externalName` is defined.

enum.description:
Enumerated value of the property.

The value of the property is limited to one of the values specified in the enum definition. The list of values for the enum must consist of unique elements.

externalName.description:
Name of the property as it exists in an external application

**NOTE**: When you add a custom property, only Identity Provider app user schemas require `externalName` to be
included in the request body. If an existing custom Identity Provider app user schema property has an empty
`externalName`, requests aren't allowed to update other properties until the `externalName` is defined.

maxLength.description: Maximum character length of a string property

minLength.description: Minimum character length of a string property

oneOf.description:
Non-empty array of valid JSON schemas. The `oneOf` key is only supported in conjunction with `enum` and provides a mechanism to return a display name for the `enum` value.
Each schema has the following format: ``` { "const": "enumValue", "title": "display name" } ``` When `enum` is used in conjunction with `oneOf`, you must keep the set of enumerated values and their order.
For exam

pattern.description: For `string` property types, specifies the regular expression used to validate the property

permissions.description: Access control permissions for the property

required.description: Determines whether the property is required

title.description: User-defined display name for the property

unique.description: Determines whether property values must be unique

required.description: A collection indicating required property names

custom.description:
All custom profile properties are defined in a profile subschema with the resolution scope `#custom`.

> **Notes:** 
> * When you refer to custom profile attributes that differ only by case, name collisions occur. This includes naming custom profile attributes the same as base profile attributes, for example, `firstName` and `FirstName`.
> * Certain attributes are reserved and can't be used for cu

required.description: A collection indicating required property names

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp when the schema was last updated

title.description: User-defined display name for the schema

_links.description: Specifies link relations (see [Web Linking](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8288)) available using the [JSON Hypertext Application Language](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-kelly-json-hal-06) specification. This object is used for dynamic discovery of related resources and lifecycle operations.

hints.description: Describes allowed HTTP verbs for the `href`

templated.description: Indicates whether the link object's `href` property is a URI template.

type.description: The media type of the link. If omitted, it is implicitly `application/json`.
